explanations.note: meanings are empirical — measured over layouts consistent with the auction under the engine's 2/1 style, not quoted from a rulebook

options.text: 1♠ — overcall: shows 9-15 HCP, 5.5 ♠ on average (n=128, measured). Next call is usually 2♦ (39%); Double (24%); Pass (18%). A strong engine chooses this 57% of the time here. Where it leads: 3NN (17%), 2DN (12%), 3CS (9%). The panel would split: 1♠, 2♣ scored within the noise (gap -0.1 IMPs, ±1.3).

2♣ — cue bid of their suit (strong raise/asking): shows 9-15 HCP, 5.2 ♠ on average, 5.2 ♥ on average (n=128, measured). Next call is usually 2♦ (41%); Pass (34%); Double (16%). A strong engine chooses this 19% of the time here. Where it leads: 4HE (22%), 2DN (16%), 4DN (9%). Scored +0.1 IMPs vs the top choice (±1.3); wins on 40% of layouts, pushes on 27%.
options.convention: cue bid of their suit (strong raise/asking)

options.text: Pass — pass: shows 5-12 HCP, balanced 82% of the time (n=128, measured). Next call is usually 1♦ (72%); 1♥ (15%); 1♠ (8%); most common continuation: 1♦ Pass Pass (61%). A strong engine chooses this 19% of the time here. Where it leads: 1DN (61%), 2DE (9%), 1HN (8%). Scored -1.2 IMPs vs the top choice (±1.1); wins on 28% of layouts, pushes on 26%.
